rationale_for_baseline_tier: Uncomplicated acute viral rhinosinusitis at day 5 with a negative orbital/intracranial screen is self-limiting; manage symptomatically with routine GP review if not improving by day 10 (T1). Severe pain warrants good analgesia, not escalation. Orbital/intracranial complications carry a hard T4/T5 safety-net.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Swelling/redness around an eye, bulging eye, double vision, painful/limited eye movements, visual change, severe unrelenting headache, neck stiffness, or confusion
escalation_edges.rationale: Orbital cellulitis or intracranial complication — sight/life-threatening emergencies.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get swelling or redness around an eye, double vision, pain moving your eyes, a severe unrelenting headache, a stiff neck or confusion — call 000 or go to the Emergency Department immediately.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Symptoms persisting beyond 10 days without improvement, or initial improvement then clear worsening (double-sickening), or high fever with facial pain
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggests bacterial rhinosinusitis warranting reassessment and possible antibiotics.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Immunocompromise/diabetes would lower the complication threshold (not reported here)
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: If disclosed, lower the threshold for review and complication screening.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Manage at home with regular pain relief, saline sinus rinses (sterile water only) and a decongestant spray for no more than 3 days. See your GP if it's not improving by day 10 or worsens after initially getting better.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Self-care with GP review by day 10; ED/000 for orbital or intracranial features
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000/ED for eye or neurological features at any time.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Acute viral rhinosinusitis day 5; complication screen negative; antibiotics withheld; reassess day 10/double-sickening.

critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Early orbital cellulitis is dismissed as 'just sinusitis' and progresses to vision loss/cavernous sinus thrombosis.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Not screening eye/neuro features before reassurance.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: ED/ambulance for uncomplicated viral sinusitis — unnecessary; also prescribing antibiotics is over-treatment.
